This neighbourhood is amazing and very vibrant. Right downstairs from the building you will find the Amsterdamse Poort with plenty of shops. You have Lidl, Albert Heijn and Vomar (for groceries), but also a local market twice a week and plenty of local stores selling groceries.

In addition you will find all sorts of other shops (H&M, Blokker) and restaurants, from cafes to KFC and McDonalds, or Asian takeaway.

If you want to relax, right next door is the gigantic Nelson Mandela park with loads of open space.

Within walking distance we are spoiled for cosy bars, small shops and supermarkets and fine dining at reasonable prices. Leave our house and turn right and in one minute you are on the Overtoom, and able to catch Tram 1 which will take you into the city center in 9 minutes. Don't worry, we will tell you how to use the trams and also help you with transport cards.

Located in the heart of Amsterdam, Frederiksplein is a charming and vibrant neighborhood that offers a wonderful mix of local living and easy access to the city's top attractions. This area, characterized by its beautiful 19th-century architecture, tree-lined streets, and close-knit community atmosphere, is an ideal base for visitors who want to experience Amsterdam like a local while staying close to major cultural landmarks and bustling shopping streets.

Family-Friendly Environment: Frederiksplein is a neighborhood that feels like home. The presence of many families, local shops, and cafes creates a welcoming environment. Proximity to Utrechtsestraat: Just around the corner, Utrechtsestraat is one of Amsterdam's most vibrant shopping streets, famous for its diverse array of unique stores.

neighborhood_overview: The Jordaan is one of the most characteristic neighborhoods of Amsterdam, located in the city center. Welcome to my neighborhood- De Pijp (The Pipe), one of the city’s most hip and trendy areas, bursting with cozy cafes, diverse restaurants and a lively nightlife.

Bohemian De Pijp's narrow streets are lined with Middle Eastern eateries, old-school pubs and cafes with sidewalk terraces. Gregarious stallholders at Albert Cuypmarkt sell Dutch specialties like herrings and waffles.

De Pijp is a foodie’s paradise. The neighborhood is home to almost 150 nationalities and the food choices certainly reflect this diversity. Casa Delea Attica (@CasaDelea) is a new, luxurious loft apartment, stylishly furnished with a spacious kitchen, bedroom with marble shower and washbasin, a double box spring bed and a separate toilet. The living area has a double sofa bed.

Casa Delea Attica (4p) and Casa Delea Cantina (2p) are located in the vibrant De Pijp district, the Quartier Latin of Amsterdam, nearby restaurants, cafés, shops, and public transport. We are happy to provide insider tips for a unique Amsterdam experience.
host_about:
Hallo,

Ik ben Ferdau Delea (Fries/Italiaans) en trotse moeder van twee kinderen. Samen wonen wij in het prachtige Amsterdam. Als Industrial Designer en Interior Designer heb ik vol enthousiasme de B&B’s vormgegeven.
Reizen is mijn grote liefde. Ik geniet ervan om de wereld te verkennen en nieuwe mensen te ontmoeten. Onze twee B&B’s, te vinden op socials onder CasaDelea, bieden gasten de kans om het beste van Amsterdam te ervaren.

Ferdau

neighborhood_overview:
The apartment is located in the Jordaan area at the centre of Amsterdam – which is undoubtly the best place to stay in this city.
The apartment is within a short distance from most of Amsterdam’s important sites and attractions, such as the Dam square, Anne Frank House, the red light district, Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh museum, Amsterdam Hermitage-museum, Westerpark (lot of nice funny and crowded festivals), and the nine streets. Furthermore, many of Amsterdam's most popular restaurants and bars are also within this range.
At 100 meters from the apartment you will find the best shopping-street in the Netherlands: the Haarlemmerstraat: Here you will find lots of nice small, authentic and funny shops, restaurants, bars, cafés, and is only 2 minutes walk from the apartment.

The Jordaan is in my opinion the best area of Amsterdam, you can find/see nearly everything here in the surroundings. If you come for a visit I will give you lots of  nice tips if you like it.  Maybe I can be your host?  You are very welcome

name: Luxury family home Amsterdam-Noord
description:
Freshly renovated 3 story, 140 m2 family home in a cosy, peaceful street in Amsterdam-Noord. The house is perfect for families with (young) children. Free parking, 10 minutes by bike to the famous NDSM Wharf, full of restaurants and cultural highlights. Central Amsterdam is just across the water, accessible via all day ferries.

The house features a huge sofa pit, a modern open kitchen, a garden with BBQ, a deluxe bathroom with double shower, 4 bedrooms and a dedicated children's bedroom.
neighborhood_overview:
Oostzanerwerf is a neighborhood in the Amsterdam North district.

Oostzanerwerf borders Kadoelen, Twiske-West, Twiske-Oost and Tuindorp Oostzaan. In addition, it is close to Landsmeer and Oostzaan. It is easy to reach by bicycle, car and bus. The neighborhood is located near nature reserve Het Twiske.

Our street has the tranquility of a village, but is also very close to the NDSM wharf. There are many nice restaurants here and you can also take the ferry to Amsterdam Central Station here.

Close to our house you can drive or cycle to the Twiske nature reserve. Here you can swim, walk, run and there are also a few nice places to have lunch or drink coffee, such as an old mill.

A 5-minute walk away you will find a great shopping center with a bakery, butcher, fishmonger, cheese shop, drugstore, and a large supermarket.

Amsterdam North is bustling with fun, hip, alternative, modern places to eat or go out.

name: Amsterdam Sleeping under Stars- Free Parking!
description: Sleeping under Stars & Free Parking! Loft, tiny apartment in restored dike house from 1903 on the Schellingwouderdijk in Amsterdam. The beautiful surroundings are a perfect combination for those who love both the City Center and Rural Life. The characteristic Family House offers views on the Amsterdam IJ and meadow for children to play, with tables from where to enjoy the panoramic sight of horses and ships sailing by. In summer swim in front skate in winter! Registered under 97188. Z/20/1761348
neighborhood_overview:
Schellingwoude is a former Fishing-village in the Northern part of Amsterdam. The Monumental Dike is situated between meadows, park and the IJ, with the City Centre just at the other side of the water. Various (sustainable/organic) grocery stores, supermarkets and bakeries are at walking distance.

A perfect area for cycling, Marken and Volendam are a 30 minute bike-ride along the dike, Edam and Monnikendam are at the distance of an hour by bike through beautiful meadows with horses, cows and sheep. One can have a break or High Tea in one of the authentic Cafes or a glass of biological milk, or biological ice-cream in a farm on the way. Excellent (sustainable) restaurants in both the rural countryside and in the artistic part of Northern Amsterdam - De Ceuvel, Pllek, Il Pecorino, Wilhelmina Dok (with views on the large cruiseships), Loetje, De Goudfazant, etc. Or one can have a cool beer or white wine on a terrace at Het Sluisje, Het Schoolhuis, Beer Brewery Oedipus, Landmark

neighborhood_overview:
When planning a visit to Amsterdam, choosing the right neighborhood to stay in can make all the difference in your experience. Nestled in the heart of Amsterdam, the area around Erasmuspark is a hidden gem that offers the perfect blend of tranquility, culture, and convenience. Here’s why this neighborhood is the best place to stay when visiting Amsterdam for a few days.

1. A Peaceful Oasis in the City
One of the greatest charms of the Erasmuspark area is its serene, green environment. The park itself is a lush haven with winding paths, tranquil ponds, and plenty of open spaces for picnics, yoga, or simply soaking in the natural beauty. Unlike the busier, tourist-heavy parts of the city, this neighborhood provides a peaceful retreat from the hustle and bustle. After a day of exploring, you can unwind with a leisurely stroll through the park or enjoy a book under a shady tree. It’s the perfect spot to relax and recharge.

2. Close Proximity to the City Center

neighborhood_overview:
Motoryacht Flint is located on a unique and beautiful location called 'Nieuwe Meer'. On the south side the lake borders on the Amsterdamse Bos. On the north side is the nature and recreation area De Oeverlanden. In about 20 minutes you can reach the centre of Amsterdam by public transport. You could say, the best of both worlds, staying in nature on the edge of the bustling city centre. Directly on the harbor is a restaurant with a large terrace that offers a beautiful view over the lake. Within walking distance of the harbor (1500 meters) you can rent bikes. Near the harbor you will find several supermarkets, which you can reach on foot but with the bike you can do your shopping faster. Within walking distance (12 minutes) is a large Wellness Resort where you can play squash, tennis or enjoy a lovely sauna.

Amsterdam Light Festival.
Every year, the Amsterdam Light Festival puts the city centre and the canals in the spotlight. From 28 November 2019 to 19 January 2020,

neighborhood_overview:
Amsterdam Oost / Amstedam East

Amsterdam East is a residential district in Amsterdam, popular under Amsterdam locals. It lies between the Amstel River in the west and the IJ in the east
The Plantage, which lies east and south-east of Waterlooplein, holds many delights, among them the Hortus Botanicus and the zoo Artis. Directly south of the Plantage is the Oosterpark, the first large park financed by the municipality of Amsterdam as it dates from 1891. The Vondelpark is older and larger, but that initially was a private project. Activities can be undertaken in the park, and a visit to the Tropenmuseum shouldn't be missed. The Dapperbuurt is known for the second largest market of Amsterdam after the Albert Cuyp Market. It has been a designated market street since 1910. Products for sale aim to a Dutch Antillean, Moroccan, Surinamese and Turkish clientele, reflecting the ethnic make-up of the area.

description: Small room with double bed in shared apartment. Modern kitchen available for home cooking if needed.
host_about:
ABOUT ME
A single Norwegian living in Amsterdam, I have a steady job and my own apartment with a guest room. I like hosting guests from all over the world, and have done so lots in the last few years. I'm a huge nerd who loves science and science fiction, and am very curious about the world around me. I like learning new things, making new friends and generally try to experience as much as I can. 

INTERESTS
I go to a gym, train kendo, do archery and pistol shooting. I like to go to concerts (all kinds of music), to cinemas (all kinds of movies), to play games (like chess or backgammon or poker, also more advanced board games), and to read books (all kinds, but sci-fi a big favorite). 

FAVORITE BOOKS
I read mostly sci-fi, fantasy and crime books, and a fair amount of non-fiction too, mostly science and atheism.

Small list of favorite fiction writers: William Gibson, Neal Stephenson, Iain M Banks, Terry Pratchett, Neil Gaiman, J. R. R. Tolkien, Alan Moore, Peter F. Hamilton, Tibor Fischer, James Ellroy, China Miéville, Moebius, Andrew Vachss, Nick Harkaway, Charles Stross, David Brin, Harry Turtledove, Ian McDonald, Patrick Rothfuss, Cory Doctorow, Tibor Fischer, Kim Stanley Robinson

Favorite non-fiction: Christopher Hitchens, Sam Harris, Richard Dawkins, Stephen Hawking, Carl Sagan. 

MOVIES
I love going to the cinema, and I love cinema from all over the world, with a special place in my heart for Japanese and Chinese films.

Small list of favorites: Bladerunner, Apocalypse Now, Full Metal Jacket, Fight Club, Hardboiled, Sonatine, Seven Samurai, Zaitochi, Once Upon A Time In The West, Akira, Ghost In The Shell, Inception, Star Wars, Alien, Terminator, Enter The Void, Stalker, Spirited Away, Princess Mononoke, Evangelion 1.0, The Usual Suspects, Seven 

MUSIC
I love all kinds of music, but especially world (ethnic), metal and rock. Weak spot for singer/songerwriters, especially female ones.

Small selection of favorites: Tom Waits, Tori Amos, Kate Bush, Bruce Springsteen, Kaizer's Orchestra, Bob Dylan, Maria Mena, Mitsoura, Johnny Cash, Ugress, Hedningarna, Ym-Stammen, Raga Rockers, Apocalyptica, Røyksopp, Faith No More, Motorpsycho, Rammstein, Jonsi, Biosphere

name: SWEETS hotel Van Hallbrug
description: Tiny and typical 1930s bridge house with lots of daylight and an amazing view. Situated in the quiet ‘Staatslieden’ neighbourhood, yet right next to the Jordaan area and close to Amsterdam Centre (15 min) and Westerpark (10 min). The surrounding neighbourhood offers an endless number of shopping, dining and sightseeing options that are worth a visit. Heads up tiny house lovers: this is SWEETS hotel’s tiniest bridge house (only 10,5 m2) with the tiniest bathroom. For curious travellers!
neighborhood_overview:
The surrounding neighbourhood of bridge house Van Hallbrug offers an endless number of shopping, dining and sightseeing options that are worth a visit – all within walking or cycling distance. Just cross the bridge and you’ll find a small square with great places to get your morning coffee (Back to Black) or a delicious vegetarian meal (Vleesch noch Visch). Westerpark, the park where locals go for a festival or picnic, is at a 10-minute walk from the bridge house. Check out our self-composed Neighbourhood Guide on the tablet in the room for more recommendations!

This bridge house is classed as NOISE LEVEL - MEDIUM.
host_about:
SWEETS hotel is a one-of-a-kind hotel located across Amsterdam in 28 different bridge houses. Each bridge house has been transformed into an independent hotel suite that accommodates a maximum of two guests.

For centuries, Amsterdam’s bridge houses accommodated the city’s many bridge keepers who were responsible for opening the impressive structures for passing water traffic. With building dates between 1673 and 2009, the bridge houses carry an extensive history. Visiting them in chronological order is like a journey through time: their various architectural styles reveal how the city developed and grew, when new quarters were built and even how people thought about city development at that time.

With the hotel room interiors, SWEETS hotel offers guests a unique and comfortable living space, while keeping the history of every bridge house in mind. All bridge houses are different, and so are the interiors. Every SWEET accommodates up to two adults and contains a kingsize double bed, a bathroom and coffee and tea facilities.

SWEETS hotel, first initiated in 2012 as an urban space project, is 8 years in the making. Over half of the 28 bridge houses are currently available for reservations, with more coming soon.

SWEETS hotel is an initiative and co-creation of the architecture office Space&Matter (known for De Ceuvel, an ecological hub), project development partner Grayfield and Suzanne Oxenaar and Otto Nan of Seven New Things, founders of Lloyd Hotel & Cultural Embassy (the world’s first 1- to 5-star hotel) and Hotel The Exchange.

description: Welcome to my charming Bed & Breakfast located in the vibrant Gerard Doustraat in the 'De Pijp' district, just a stone's throw away from the famous Albert Cuyp Market. Discover the enchanting charm of Old Pijp in Amsterdam. This historic neighborhood exudes authenticity, boasting picturesque streets, numerous delightful restaurants, cafes, and terraces, along with Amsterdam's most famous local market. Immerse yourself in a vibrant blend of culture and conviviality in the heart of Amsterdam!

The strategic location provides easy access to major attractions such as the Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh Museum, the Heineken Experience, and, of course, the picturesque canals. Experience authentic Amsterdam life during your stay at my Bed & Breakfast, where comfort and location come together for an unforgettable experience. Book now and discover the best of De Pijp!

Welcome,

I'm Felix, and I look forward welcoming you to my B&B in De Pijp, Amsterdam.

Having lived my whole life in Amsterdam, I enjoy all aspects the city has to offer. I know every little corner and all the places. Don't feel shy to ask!

I embrace new experiences and storytelling, whether as a guest exploring or as host welcoming others. Beauty lies in encounters along the way.

Excited welcoming you to vibrant Amsterdam!
Warm regards, Felix

neighborhood_overview:
The Jordaan is one of the oldest districts of Amsterdam and where the real”Amsterdammer” are from. The best features of the city are within walking distance, making it easier to get around, Tram,bus parking are very close to the Hotel.
Locals will recommend that you take a stroll along the canals in the evening. It is fabulous.
The famous 9 streets (9 Straatjes) is only a 2-minute walk away. Here you will find all kinds of small shops, cafes and restaurants. The lively Leidseplein where all Bars / Cafe and Nightlife, is 300m away, you could bike to the red light districts or take a lovely stroll through the city. The Elandsgracht is a 2-minute walk away, here you can find good restaurants and supermarkets. A 5-minute walk away is Museumplein, the area that houses the Rijksmuseum, stedelijk Museum or the Van Gogh Museum. name: Old schoolbuilding apartment
description:
Welcome to Amsterdam-Noord! Our house is a stylish loft apartment (145m2) located at the ground floor of a renovated school building with a large garden & garden house.

Characterized by a high ceiling and high windows that bring bundles of light during the day. In the evenings you can retract on the entresol to watch read, watch television or relax. Or enjoy the garden house for a nice barbeque and some outdoor feel. The garden house (30 m2) offers an additional sofa bed and a desk.
neighborhood_overview:
Amsterdam Noord is the creative hotspot of the Dutch Capital with many restaurants, creative hotspots and special sights.
Our house is located in a quiet neighborhood of Amsterdam Noord, surrounded by a park and forest. All the bustling of Amsterdam-Noord and the rest of the city nearby, but with a nice balance of nature.

It's a small village area within Amsterdam but only a 4 minute walk to the metro station which can take you to the other side of the city within 15 minutes (including different other stops of course). There is also enough space for car parking if required.

If you go opposite of the metro station, you'll find yourself in either a park which is great for picnics, or the woods. That's just lovely for a morning stroll!

There are multiple shops in walking distance, to get your day to day groceries.

neighborhood_overview:
The Amsterdam Canal District is a neighborhood in Amsterdam, Netherlands. The seventeenth-century canals of Amsterdam, located in the center of Amsterdam, were added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in August 2010. The Amsterdam Canal District consists of the area around the city’s four main canals: Singel, Herengracht, Keizersgracht, and Prinsengracht. From Brouwersgracht, the canals are generally parallel with one another, leading gradually southeast into the Amstel river.

Many of the canal houses in the Amsterdam Canal District are from the Dutch Golden Age in the 18th century. Many of these buildings, however, underwent restoration or reconstruction in various centuries, meaning that these building display many different architectural styles and facades.

neighborhood_overview:
Our house is right on the edge of the city centre, in the OOSTERPARK area, with it's own vibe and sparkle.
We are close to the zoo (Artis) and just around the corner you find the Oosterpark, the Tropen Museum, relaxed bars (Javastreet area) good food and the famous Windmill Brewery 't IJ.

host_about:
Once I stayed in a tiny room in the heart of Tribeca, NYC. Outside the window, squashed between brick walls stood a tall tree "planted by Yoko Ono". A memorable sales pitch for a queen-size bed on top of the world. 
Living on Wooster Street I was a local for a week, a fantastic experience. I would love to offer this kind of experience to my guests when staying in Amsterdam.

The house is mainly occupied by me, my son and Bonnie the cat, but we love to entertain friends and family with food and stories, so the house can be quiet as well as very lively. A family home in short ;-)

Our room for rent is on the second floor of a 4 story house, and with it's own bathroom on the same floor, it is all rather private and relaxed, for the guests as well as us. We live on the two top floors, just a shout away if any help is needed. 
My personal interests are art, photography, film and design, and I love to show my guests the local hotspots. New shows and exhibitions, exciting places to go for shopping, coffee or food, I am happy to share if you want to know.
 
I travelled west as far as San Francisco and east as far as Varanasi. Both ways there was always a 'friend's connection' to help out with local stuff. This was before Airbnb existed, and now through this organization I can maybe be that connection for you.
Happy to help.

In daily life I am a professional creative.

description:
Discover a new kind of business hotel in the heart of the canal district.

Located within 1 mile of Amsterdam Central Station, Zoku is designed for professionals, business travelers, and remote workers who are on the lookout for a trendy & sustainable apartment hotel for 1 day, to 1 month, to 1 year.

When you feel like leaving your private Loft to socialize, the rooftop Social Spaces are open 24/7 and cater to your fun, practical, and professional needs - all while providing amazing views!
neighborhood_overview:
Located in the heart of Amsterdam, Zoku Amsterdam is a 3-minute walk from the historic canal district. You can explore the beautiful canals in walking distance to the center and gaze upon the famous Skinny Bridge right from their shared rooftop. In just 1km you can also reach Oosterpark, the perfect place to take in some nature on your next run.

The surrounding Plantage neighborhood is home to plenty of cozy cafes, restaurants, and local sites including:

• 350m "Magere Brug" Skinny Bridge
• 500m Hermitage (Amsterdam History Museum)
• 600m Hortus Botanicus (Botanical Garden)
• 650m Artis (Zoo)
• 1km Oosterpark
• 1.7km Centraal Station

The building is located on "Weesperstraat", a street easily accessible by car, bicycle, and public transport (only a 3-minute walk to the metro, bus, and tram). Being 10 minutes away from the Central Station by metro and 15 minutes from the city's business districts, the "RAI" and the "Zuidas",
host_about: We’re Zoku. Designed for the new generation of business travelers, we’re so much more than a long-stay hotel. We’re a place you can call your second home, even on your first visit. We’ve created a hive of private Lofts and rooftop Social Spaces, so you can stay connected and well-rested as you live and work throughout Europe. Throw yourself into your work and play, and effortlessly discover one of our home cities. It will be your home city next.

name: Authentic Farmhouse The Vergulden Eenhoorn 1
description:
There is great history behind the hotel. The Vergulden Eenhoorn was established in 1702 as a city farm and used to be one of very few farmhouses the city of Amsterdam has.

Together with the city of Amsterdam the farmhouse was fully renovated to modern standards, while maintaining the historic value and its features.

The space is fully equipped with all necessities to make your stay as comfortable as possible!
neighborhood_overview: Amsterdam East is a very populair neighborhood. The hotel and restaurant are a stones throw away from the Amstel Canal, where you can go for a nice walk or visit one of the bars and restaurants with water side views. Within a 10 min walk you will find park Frankendael and other hotspots of Amsterdam East.

name: BnJoy 4Pers/ Clean, Cozy & Central/ Stunning View!
description:
Two comfy and clean private bedrooms in a authentic canal house with amazing views. In the common hallway, there is a bathroom and a separate toilet to share. A kitchenette offers the possibility to make some coffee or tea, a small breakfast, or a nighttime snack.

Only 5 minutes walking distance from the central station with public transport in all directions. Attractions like China town, Red Light District, Dam Square, and the famous 9 Streets are within walking distance.

Also Bikes 4 Rent!
neighborhood_overview:
Welcome to the heart of the vibrant city!
Soak up everything the center of Amsterdam has to offer. At the same time enjoy the relaxed neighborhood, only 5 minutes away from it all.

If you go outside to the left you go downtown, straight into the numerous attractions like the Red Light District, China Town, Dam Square, and the famous 9 Streets. Walk to the right and you'll enter the relaxed and old-styled Amsterdam, stroll down the various streets, and don't be shy to peak into the houses. The Waterloo square with Amsterdam's oldest flea market is something you don't want to miss!

It's also very easy from here to explore the rest of the country, starting from the central station which you will reach whiting 5 minutes walking distance. In front of the building, there is a bus stop that will take you to idyllic neighboring villages like 'Broek in Waterland'and the famous 'Volendam'.

The choice is yours.
host_about:
HI! 

My name is Roy and I live in this beautiful canal house in the vibrant heart of Amsterdam which I'm happy to share with you. It is super comfy, centrally located, and has a stunning view over the 'Oosterdok' water.

The B&B fits excellent for 4 persons, friends or couples. All relaxed and positive humans who enjoy life. You can expect some inside information and local tips to make the most of your stay.

Looking forward to meeting you!

name: Private Houseboat~City Center
description:
Our beautiful ship is one of the rare ships who has a quay and is located in the centre of Amsterdam. It's like a oasis in the city: it's quiet, almost no traffic, but 150 meters further and you are in the centre of the jordaan. Haarlemmerstraat, which was called 'best shopping street of Holland', is at 150 meters.

Also nice to know: It's possible to jump from the ship and swim...

All in all the perfect Amsterdam experience!
neighborhood_overview:
We think the best thing about our ship is the fact that it's in the center of Amsterdam, but not on the busy canals. So you won't be bothered by people on little or big boats that will float by. The ship is situated in the oldest part of Amsterdam, with the old canalhouses where Amsterdam is famous for. If you walk underneath the railway track, you're immediately in the busy streets of Amsterdam: the Jordaan.
A big supermarket is at 200 feet distance and all sorts of nice shops in the Haarlemmerstraat (best shopping street of Holland 2012).
We have a bicycle which you can use, so you can see all of Amsterdam.
host_about:
We are a family of three, living on our ship in Amsterdam. If you have any special requests for your stay or need information on places to go, eat, drink, please do not hesitate to drop us a line. We will try to answer you as best as we can to make your stay as pleasant and comfortable as possible.

Our ship was build in 1899 and originally used for transportation. Many years ago it was transformed into a beautiful houseboat and now located in the centre of Amsterdam. The Haarlemmerstraat, which was called the best shopping street in The Netherlands in 2012, is 300 meters away. Central Station is within walking distance.
Our ship is docked in the oldest part of Amsterdam: Prinseneiland / Jordaan. 
We have a quay, next to the ship, where you can relax in the sun, till late in the evening. We also have a fire pit at the quay, so you can extend those lovely nights....

We have a big kitchen, with dishwasher, microwave, freezer, etc.
The livingroom is big as well, we have a television, wifi, WII and a wood stove.
In the front of the ship is one (large) bedroom with a bed for two persons and in the back of the ship is also a bed for two persons, a little bit bigger than the other bed.
We have a bathroom with a bathtub and shower.
Washing machine is also available on the ship.
We have two cats. They are both lovely and will stay on the ship.

We have a natural fence of plants around the water, so it is quite safe for little kids. But we also have life vests for the little ones, which is advised.

The ship is docked and not able to sail anymore, so you don't have to be afraid of waking up in the middle of the canal ;)

All in all a great opportunity to experience the Dutch live on a houseboat!

name: Eco-friendly 2 Single Beds Room by Vondelpark
neighborhood_overview: Conscious Hotel The Tire Station is located right next to the Vondelpark. By bike you only have to cruise through the park and you find yourself at the Leidse square in no time. In this area there are the most famous museums of Amsterdam, like the Rijksmuseum, the Van Gogh Museum and the Stedelijk Museum. host_about:
We are conscious about the choices we make. That is why there is sustainability in everything that we do, without compromising comfort. And our stylish hotels and restaurants each have their own unique style and story, which makes every visit a special experience. We do it differently by nature.

We have been around for over 10 years now, making us pioneers in eco-friendly tourism.

We do it differently by combining stylish design and playfulness with sustainability, calling it Eco-Sexy. This is felt throughout your entire stay. From small notes in the rooms that tell guests about the recycled furniture and water-saving showers to every bite and drink that are served being fully organic. There is no stopping us delivering the eco choice while maintaining a high level of comfort.

name: Queensized Bunk Bed in 6 Bed
description:
Enjoy some extra leg room with one of our Queensized bunks within a cosy shared dorm at the Flying Pig Downtown Hostel. Our 18th century building has been transformed into one of the most notorious party spots in the Dutch capital. Complete with reading lights, USB ports and lockers under your bed to store your things, this is the perfect spot for young backpackers to base themselves.

This listing offers 1 Bed in a bunk, which sleeps up to 2 guests.
host_about:
Our Flying Pig Downtown hostel is the place to party in Amsterdam with the 18th century building having been transformed into one of the most notorious party spots in the Dutch capital. We host awesome DJ’s and epic themed parties boasting an indoor smoking area with a pillow stage! 

Located in the heart of the city centre next to Amsterdam Central Station, you will also find Dam Square, The Anne Frank House and the bustling Red Light District just a short stroll away. Our hostel also provides free use of the guest kitchen, luggage storage, free Wi-Fi, a book exchange and a buffet style continental breakfast available everyday for only €5!

Known for our wild nights and a relaxed atmosphere during the day, we’re a popular spot for young backpackers to base themselves. There’s a reason we were rated the Best Hostel in the Netherlands in 2013 by TripAdvisor!

This is our home in Amsterdam and we are very proud of it and sure you will have a wonderful stay and time in Amsterdam. Last year we entirely renovated the apartment, and now its fully ready to start welcoming guests.

It features a spacious living room, open kitchen, two bedrooms, modern bathroom with 2 person jacuzzi, and a large terrace. The master bedroom has A/C. We're sure you'll have a wonderfull time and stay at our place! Prepare for the trip of a lifetime...
neighborhood_overview:
The apartment is in the neighbourhood: ‘De Baarsjes’ next to Westerpark and Oud-West.

★ Around the corner several shops and supermarkets (open from 8 till 22)
★ Albert Heijn or Aldi supermarket both a 5 minute walk.
★ Foodhallen (famous foodmarket) just 10 minute walk.
★ Jordaan (old city-center) just a 15 minute walk
★ Erasmuspark around the corner
★ And a lot of bars and restaurants just a couple of streets away.

Go shopping at the Jan Evertsenstraat and check out the ‘Balconie’ plant shop. ‘Things I like and Things I love’ is worth a stop. You definitly see something nice.

At the end you have a great fish shop, try a ‘herring’! You can’t go more Dutch then that.

BOS EN LOMMER & DE BAARSJES
Boasting cool cultural establishments, green open spaces and a young, and multicultural crowd, Bos en Lommer and Baarsjes is rapidly expanding into one of Amsterdam's most dynamic creative outposts.
Bos en Lommer

neighborhood_overview:
Amsterdam Canal District is a neighborhood in Amsterdam, Netherlands. The seventeenth-century canals in Amsterdam, located in the center of Amsterdam, were added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in August 2010. The Amsterdam Canal District consists of the area around the city's four main canals: Singel, Herengracht, Keizersgracht and Prinsengracht. From Brouwersgracht, the canals are generally parallel to each other, and gradually lead southeast into the Amstel River.

Many of the canal houses in the Amsterdam Canal District are from the Dutch Golden Age in the 18th century. However, many of these buildings underwent restoration or reconstruction in different centuries, which means that these buildings display many different architectural styles and facades.

The Spiegelkwartier is the Art and Antiques district in Amsterdam.
Here you’ll find more than 70 antiques dealers and art galleries, together offering a resplendent array of fine historical objects, classic and contemporary art, and exotic folk art.
You will find also restaurants, casual eateries and cosy cafés on almost every corner.
The Spiegelkwartier is located between the Herengracht and the Rijksmuseum.

www.spiegelkwartier.nl

Museum Quarter

The Amsterdam Museum Quarter is home to the three most important museums in the Netherlands: the Rijksmuseum, the Stedelijk Museum and the Van Gogh Museum.
It also houses the majestic Concertgebouw and some superb cafés and restaurants.
With its large grassy open area, the Museumplein offers plenty of room to have a picnic or just relax. The Museum Quarter even has its own indoor swimming pool.

neighborhood_overview:
The Ark is located at the Weesperzijde one of the most special places near the Amstel which is according to many guest their favorite area of Amsterdam. This part is very popular because of its many different bars and restaurants and the nice atmosphere. Also the Weesperzijde is known for having the most sun hours a day in the city.

At the other side of the Amstel there is another popular part of the city, De Pijp – like Quartier Latin in Paris. There you will also find many lovely places to eat. From the residence you are able to go on a wounderful walk alongside the Amstel where you will pass many famous places such as the Stopera, Hermitage, Carre Theatre, Amstel Hotel and finally the Amstel Tower. Also you will pass by all kinds of popular terraces like the famous and typically Amsterdam, Cafe Hesp. Finally, if you would like to have some activity you will also have the opportunity to rent tennis gear or a kano.

I am Masha and have two kids Boaz (5) & Luca (3,5).
I live on the Amstel for 8 years. First we lived at a real boat, a sailing ship. We were in love of this place because of the oase of peace & nature in the middle of the city. But because of kids coming the ship became too small for 2 kids, so we started to realise an ark, this took about 1,5 year. On the 1st birthday of our daugther Luca this Ark was shipped to this place and still we are enjoying and we even love this place more and more.

neighborhood_overview:
The Jordaan neighbourhood is tucked inside central Amsterdam and quite conveniently located. Arguably Amsterdam's most famous district, the area contains an upscale assortment of art galleries, patio restaurants, cafes and street musicians.
Trendy Jordaan's narrow canals and streets are flanked by indie boutiques, cosy pubs, and hip eateries. Stalls at the Noordermarkt square market offer jewellery, clothes, antiques and organic food. Antiques Centre Amsterdam sells vintage ceramics and paintings, while smaller galleries in the area focus on innovative contemporary art. Other attractions include the Houseboat Museum and the Anne Frank Huse!

name: Big house close to NDSM, 2 cats. Max 4 people.
description:
PLEASE NO LOUD MUSIC OR DISTURBANCE.
Because of the rules in Amsterdam no more than 4 guests.
Our house is close to the center of Amsterdam and the trendy NDSM-wharf. The house is ideal for young families, couples or small quiet groups. neighborhood_overview:
Our house is situated in a quiet residential area in Amsterdam-North. There are not many tourists here, but with the (free) ferry you are in downtown Amsterdam within 20 minutes. An excellent place to escape the crowdy parts to make the most of your stay.

Practical information:
* There is a supermarket at walking distance. It is called Plus and you’ll find it at the end of the street. It’s open till 9 P.M. More supermarkets and food shops you’ll find at the mall called Banne Buiksloot or Molenwijk, which is a 20 minutes’ walk. For the best bread & fresh croissants you can go to a nice bakery called BROOD which is located near the NDSM ferry. There is also a weekly market on the Van Der Pekstraat (wed, fri & sat). Here you can buy cheap, fresh products. For the market take bus 35 till central station, bus stop Mosplein.

Things to do and see:
In Amsterdam-North:
* You are close to the NDSM wharf, where you will find a couple of restaurants and bars

name: Hotel Not Hotel Amsterdam: Poison Ivy
neighborhood_overview: Traditionally a blue collar neighbourhood just a stone's throw away from the heart of town, 'De Baarsjes', is what one calls 'on the rise'. All around us we see new initiatives popping up; from intimate coffee bars and restaurants, to cool boutiques with local designers. Please ask the staff for inside info on where to go!
host_about:
Hotel Not Hotel Amsterdam is a place where a room isn’t just a room, but a work of art with its own creator, story, and identity. Most of them designed and specially made by Collaboration-O, a group of young designers that originated from the Eindhoven Design Academy, and one by Arno Coenen and his partner IRIS.

When you wake up, you step into an art-filled, exhibition-like space instead of an anonymous lobby or hallway. The entire hotel is one big living room, with cosy seating areas between the cottages. Combined with our luxury beds and other facilities, Hotel Not Hotel offers an unrivalled experience. 
Besides all this, we feel that the value of a city lies in experiencing its local day-to-day life. As we are closely connected to Amsterdam and its numerous creative entrepreneurs, we know where to send you for a night on the town, a hidden culinary gem, or an upcoming artist’s exhibition. 

The bar connected to Hotel Not Hotel is called Kevin Bacon. Just like the actor, the bar has zero pretention, it always delivers, and it makes sure everybody has a good time.

Kevin Bacon is not your typical hotel bar. It’s where locals get their morning coffee or a quick breakfast. It’s where friends meet for a cocktail or a beer before diving into the nightlife, and where hotel guests let their hair down after a long day in the city.

When the weather is good, the ‘Whitey the White’ terrace is sunny like a perfectly fried egg. The staff’s friendly attitude shines all year round.

We'd like to share our beautiful place with you. We have really tried to make it ours, using lots of colours and almost only second hand furniture. It is a very bright space, with large windows and quite spacious for the two of us living here.

It is located in Bolo which is short for Bos en Lommer, a part of the West of Amsterdam, with multiple options for public transport to the city center. The neighbourhood is lively, but definitely not touristy. You'll have a peaceful night sleep here.
host_about:
I was born and raised in Amsterdam and have lived everywhere in the city. I love my city and have a ton of recommendations if you want any! I like sports a lot, my main interests right now are yoga, swimming and going the gym a couple of times a week. I also teach yoga at a gym in Amstelveen. 

When I'm travelling I usually go surfing or explore a city. I haven't traveled nearly enough, but I have seen quite some cities in Europe. Still much more to see though!

Amsterdam's Red Light District
The majority of people have heard about Amsterdam’s Red Light District well before their visit. Leaving nothing to the imagination, some stereotypes about this area are true...

There are plenty of sex shops, peep shows, brothels, an elaborate condom shop, a sex museum and prostitutes in red-lit windows. But the heart of Amsterdam is much more than that. New opportunities are setting in place a future for the city centre that will show the many qualities.

Let's be honest
Prostitution has enjoyed a long tradition of tolerance in Amsterdam. Safety is key here. In addition to preventing forced prostitution, the aim is an open and honest approach. Sex-workers here have their own union, plenty of police protection, an information centre (for visitors as well), frequent monitoring and testing and professional standards.

neighborhood_overview:
AMSTERDAM NINE STREETS - DE NEGEN STRAATJES
The quaint and quirky streets that straddle Amsterdam’s grandest canals form the city’s most photogenic micro-neighborhood. Known to locals as De Negen Straatjes or ‘The Nine Streets’, this special neighborhood is full to bursting with vintage and designer shopping, specialty stores and cozy cafés.

If you ask us the Nine Streets are the most cosy streets of Amsterdam. You find them within walking distance of the Dam Square at the western part of the Canal District. Like the name suggests, the district is famous for its nine streets connected by the canals. Here you will find great fashion boutiques and delicious lunch cafes. The Nine Streets are a must visit for everyone living in- or visiting Amsterdam.

The Nine Streets consist of: Reestraat, Hartenstraat, Gasthuismolensteeg, Berenstraat, Wolvenstraat, Oude Spiegelstraat, Runstraat, Huidenstraat and Wijde Heisteeg.

neighborhood_overview:
Our apartment is located in the famous and traditional Dutch quarter called Jordaan. In the corner you will find the Prinsengracht (Prince's canal) where starts the main city center. On the same canal, 200 metres from the apartment, you'll find the Anne Frank house.

Jordaan is without doubt the most famous Amsterdam quarter. Like the well known London Cockneys, this neighborhood was a stronghold of the working class and was famous for its strong community bonds, its radical politics and the people drinking and singing together. Nowadays the area is famous of it's small canals, scenic streets galleries, restaurants and specialty shops. Undoubtedly the neighbourhood breathes a characteristic old Amsterdam atmosphere.

- Red Light District (5 min)

description: 23 SouS is centrally located in the old part of the center. Our 18th-century house offers two comfortable double rooms, each with a private entrance and a private bathroom. For business travelers, conference venues are easily accessible from 23 SouS.
neighborhood_overview:
Popular points of interest within walking distance include Royal Palace Amsterdam, Canals, Hermitage, and The Old Church. The Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh Museum, Stedelijk Museum and Anne Frank Museum are 15 minutes by tram 7.

Especially in this part of the center there are many family activities to do with children within walking distance, such as the zoo Artis and a unique public swimming spot in the canal behind the Maritime Museum. So take your swimwear with you! Museums for the whole family within walking distance are the Maritime Museum, Nemo, Rembrandthuis, Jewish Historical Museum, and Tropenmuseum.

For business travelers, conference venues, such as the BIMhuis, are easily accessible from 23 SouS. Congress center Rai can be reached in 18 minutes via the central station with bus 22 and metro.

The Baarsjes (part of Amsterdam West) is a trendy neighbourhood with a population that is both young and multicultural. Situated remarkably close to the city centre and with plenty of green, the area is attractive for families, artists and young professionals alike.

This apartment is situated in the Orteliusstraat. With plenty of shops, bars and restaurants in the neighbourhood, you won't have to go far to find what you need. This quiet neighbourhood is situated at walking distance of the Rembrandpark and Erasmuspark. Famous Vondelpark is within 10 minutes (bike)distance. The famous Jordaan area, with its “Negen straatjes" (9 streets) - home to many of Amsterdam's exciting smaller boutiques, is just a few stops away. The Palace on Dam Square or Anne Frank house is also within easy reach, either by bike or tram.

name: Hotel Not Hotel: Secret Bookcase Whitey the White
description: Secret Bookcase with a view on one of Amsterdam's most infamous streets - the Whitey the White-street.
neighborhood_overview: Traditionally a blue collar neighbourhood just a stone's throw away from the heart of town, 'De Baarsjes', is what one calls 'on the rise'. All around us we see new initiatives popping up; from intimate coffee bars and restaurants, to cool boutiques with local designers. Please ask the staff for inside info on where to go!
host_about:
Hotel Not Hotel Amsterdam is a place where a room isn’t just a room, but a work of art with its own creator, story, and identity. Most of them designed and specially made by Collaboration-O, a group of young designers that originated from the Eindhoven Design Academy, and one by Arno Coenen and his partner IRIS.

When you wake up, you step into an art-filled, exhibition-like space instead of an anonymous lobby or hallway. The entire hotel is one big living room, with cosy seating areas between the cottages. Combined with our luxury beds and other facilities, Hotel Not Hotel offers an unrivalled experience. 
Besides all this, we feel that the value of a city lies in experiencing its local day-to-day life. As we are closely connected to Amsterdam and its numerous creative entrepreneurs, we know where to send you for a night on the town, a hidden culinary gem, or an upcoming artist’s exhibition. 

The bar connected to Hotel Not Hotel is called Kevin Bacon. Just like the actor, the bar has zero pretention, it always delivers, and it makes sure everybody has a good time.

Kevin Bacon is not your typical hotel bar. It’s where locals get their morning coffee or a quick breakfast. It’s where friends meet for a cocktail or a beer before diving into the nightlife, and where hotel guests let their hair down after a long day in the city.

When the weather is good, the ‘Whitey the White’ terrace is sunny like a perfectly fried egg. The staff’s friendly attitude shines all year round.

description:

The house boat is fully kids safe and suitable for families. Swimming in the water, or bycicling to the recreational area Oeverlanden and Lake the Nieuwe Meer for more swimming. The Northsea Beach is 30 mins by car.

No groups and we prefer families
neighborhood_overview:
The house boat is located in a quit area on the Ringvaart van de Haarlemmermeer on the outskirts of Amsterdam. If you want the bustling city life this isn't the place to be. It is approximately 20-25 minutes by bike from the Vondelpark and public transportation is available into the centre of town.

The Ringvaartdijk is a dead end street with only local car traffic. There are a lot of pedestrians/runners and byciclists using it as it connects and is close recreational areas (Oeverlanden, Nieuwe Meer). The Amsterdamse bos is only a ferry ride away and great to take kids on a bicycle tour.

Going into the city is convenient by bike and the bike lanes are fully separated from the car lanes all the way into the city centre which makes it more safe.

neighborhood_overview: Houthaven is a neighbourhood of Amsterdam, Netherlands situated in the West borough. Literally meaning "lumber port", Houthaven is a port situated along the IJ in Amsterdam's western port. It is bordered by a dam in the IJ river to the north and the Spaarndammerbuurt in the south. It is currently predominantly used by inland barges, however plans are in motion to radically change the area in the next few years by building a new residential area on seven artificial islands.

name: Hotel Not Hotel Amsterdam: Secret Bookcase Medium
neighborhood_overview: Traditionally a blue collar neighbourhood just a stone's throw away from the heart of town, 'De Baarsjes', is what one calls 'on the rise'. All around us we see new initiatives popping up; from intimate coffee bars and restaurants, to cool boutiques with local designers. Please ask the staff for inside info on where to go!
host_about:
Hotel Not Hotel Amsterdam is a place where a room isn’t just a room, but a work of art with its own creator, story, and identity. Most of them designed and specially made by Collaboration-O, a group of young designers that originated from the Eindhoven Design Academy, and one by Arno Coenen and his partner IRIS.

When you wake up, you step into an art-filled, exhibition-like space instead of an anonymous lobby or hallway. The entire hotel is one big living room, with cosy seating areas between the cottages. Combined with our luxury beds and other facilities, Hotel Not Hotel offers an unrivalled experience. 
Besides all this, we feel that the value of a city lies in experiencing its local day-to-day life. As we are closely connected to Amsterdam and its numerous creative entrepreneurs, we know where to send you for a night on the town, a hidden culinary gem, or an upcoming artist’s exhibition. 

The bar connected to Hotel Not Hotel is called Kevin Bacon. Just like the actor, the bar has zero pretention, it always delivers, and it makes sure everybody has a good time.

Kevin Bacon is not your typical hotel bar. It’s where locals get their morning coffee or a quick breakfast. It’s where friends meet for a cocktail or a beer before diving into the nightlife, and where hotel guests let their hair down after a long day in the city.

When the weather is good, the ‘Whitey the White’ terrace is sunny like a perfectly fried egg. The staff’s friendly attitude shines all year round.

neighborhood_overview:
De Pijp (Dutch pronunciation: [də ˈpɛip]; English: The Pipe) is a neighbourhood of Amsterdam, Netherlands. It is located directly south of Amsterdam's city centre and it is part of the borough Amsterdam-Zuid, in a part of the city known as the Old South (Oud Zuid). It is served by De Pijp metro station. Most streets in De Pijp are named after Dutch painters, like Jan Steen, Frans Hals, Ruysdael and Vincent van Gogh. Diamantbuurt, Nieuwe Pijp and Oude Pijp are the three districts composing the area.

The busiest street market of the Netherlands, the Albert Cuyp Market, is located in De Pijp. It is open six days per week and attracts tourists. The former Heineken brewery is also a local tourist attraction; the former town hall of Nieuwer-Amstel is one of De Pijp's most notable monuments. Next to the former Heineken brewery is the Marie Heinekenplein, which has a number of bars and cafes. Along the canal Ruysdaelkade, there is a small red-light district.

A short walk to the famous Vondelpark, located in the heart of the vibrant Oud-West/ De Baarsjes neighbourhood. You will find loads of shops, restaurants & playgrounds. A perfect base to explore Amsterdam with your family.

The apartment has beautiful canal view which you will enjoy hugely. Also a 30 square meter patio filled with plants and trees, perfect for outdoor dining or drinks. Very kids friendly.
neighborhood_overview:
Within walking distance of both Museumplein and Leidseplein, and with the adjacent Vondelpark, the Oud-West/ De Baarsjes district is a diverse, densely populated and central part of the city. It roughly covers the length of the Overtoom, a major thoroughfare, the length of Kinkerstraat, a major shopping street and the areas between these two parallel streets.

With a population of about 70,000 inhabitants it has - together with Westerpark, De Baarsjes and Bos en Lommer - merged into the new city borough of Amsterdam West.

Highlights
The main attractions in Amsterdam Oud-West are De Hallen - a cultural and culinary centre in old tram depot - and the Vondelpark - the largest city park. Also the Rembrandtpark and Erasmuspark a worth a visit if you a bit more local atmosphere. The "Jordaan" and the Museum Quarter are only a walk away.

neighborhood_overview:
Discover the Vibrant Neighborhood of De Pijp, Amsterdam

Located in the heart of Amsterdam's lively De Pijp district, this area offers the perfect blend of tranquility and city buzz. Known for its bohemian charm, De Pijp is one of Amsterdam’s most desirable neighborhoods, offering a vibrant mix of culture, cuisine, and character.

What Makes De Pijp Special?
• Albert Cuyp Market: This iconic market is a paradise for food lovers, with fresh produce, street food, and unique finds.
• Boutique Shopping: Explore quirky shops and independent boutiques lining the streets.
• Diverse Dining: From traditional Dutch cafes to international cuisine, De Pijp is a haven for food enthusiasts.
• Green Escape: Relax in nearby Sarphatipark, a peaceful oasis for a morning jog or a leisurely picnic.
• Art & Culture: Enjoy the vibrant art scene and cultural landmarks like the Heineken Experience and the Rijksmuseum,
all within walking distance.

name: Clean comfy private studio in De Pijp central
description:
Beautiful and characteristic 1-room B&B studio with private bathroom, toilet and kitchenette on the second floor of an old Dutch building (1886) in vibrant neighbourhood 'De Pijp', central Amsterdam.

RAI conference center can easily be reached within 15 minutes.

The studio can be reached by the communal central staircase of the house.

This studio is for two people only and not suitable for children and infants under 12 years old.

The property has solar panels and is listed energy label A.
neighborhood_overview:
The studio is located perfectly in vibrant area 'De Pijp'. On the left is the romantic Sarphatipark, with in its 'Liefdesbruggetje' (lovers bridge).
Since 1880 lovers would show each other, and the neighbourhood, that they are an item. To the right is the Albert Cuyp market, Hollands longest market.

'De Pijp' is without doubt a melting pot of cultures and nationalities. It is brimming with fantastic cafés, culinary restaurants, coffee shops, supermarkets and bars. A heaven for foodies!
The nearest supermarket, Albert Heijn, is a 2 minute walk.

Within walking distance from the studio you will find popular sites such as the Heineken Museum, the Van Gogh Museum, The Stedelijk museum, Vondelpark, The Royal Concert hall, Paradiso, Leidseplein and the Rijksmuseum. There is also a canal cruise dock opposite the Heineken Museum. The city center is a 15 minute walk away.

The studio is also close to RAI conference center.

Please have a look
host_about:
I love to travel and I know how important it is to have a comfortable, relaxing accommodation abroad for a fresh start to the day and a good nights sleep at night.

De Pijp is my favourite area of Amsterdam, I hope you enjoy it as much as I do :)

My partner and I welcome you to our home and are happy to help in any way we can. Just ask!

name: Spacious modern house with cat.
description:
Our spacious, stylishly decorated house is fully furnished. With a south-facing garden, a brand new kitchen, a bubble bath and a fully equipped study you will enjoy Amsterdam to the fullest. Staying here is a perfect mix of nature and hustle and bustle, with the NDSM (industrial art-district) just steps away and nature reserve Twiske around the corner.

Our house is a real residence, so not just for rental. That means that our super sweet, cuddly cat Jip also likes to be fed and taken care of!
neighborhood_overview:
The Tuindorp-Oostzaan neighborhood truly has "the best of both worlds". The bustling NDSM with its street art, art studios, fun bars and flea markets is just around the corner (5 to 10 min). Here you also take the ferry to the center within 10 min. So if you like, you are right in the buzz of the city, but on the other hand you can also get a breath of fresh air in the Twiske nature reserve. Enjoy a typical Dutch rural view with windmills. You can enjoy hiking and swimming in the summer.

Furthermore, our house is not far from the ring road and you can park for free. One of the few places in Amsterdam where that is still possible. The bus also stops in front of the door and has a good connection to Schiphol Airport and Central Station.

host_about: Generator Amsterdam is located in the up-and-coming eastern side of the city, overlooking Oosterpark. This former zoological university building can accommodate 564 guests in 168 twin and quadruple rooms, with en-suite bathrooms and incredible views of the surrounding area. There is also one luxury apartment for six people, with views of the park and a self-catered kitchen. It has flexible and spacious social areas, filled with creative design touches like glass-fronted elevators, a light and airy café and an outdoor terrace. We’ve turned the former lecture hall into an impressive chill-out lounge and bar, while the old library is now a relaxation and private events room. The basement and café are now dining destinations and the original boiler room has become a secret late night bar.

name: Hotel Not Hotel Ams: Invisible Room Downstairs
neighborhood_overview: Traditionally a blue collar neighbourhood just a stone's throw away from the heart of town, 'De Baarsjes', is what one calls 'on the rise'. All around us we see new initiatives popping up; from intimate coffee bars and restaurants, to cool boutiques with local designers. Please ask the staff for inside info on where to go!
host_about:
Hotel Not Hotel Amsterdam is a place where a room isn’t just a room, but a work of art with its own creator, story, and identity. Most of them designed and specially made by Collaboration-O, a group of young designers that originated from the Eindhoven Design Academy, and one by Arno Coenen and his partner IRIS.

When you wake up, you step into an art-filled, exhibition-like space instead of an anonymous lobby or hallway. The entire hotel is one big living room, with cosy seating areas between the cottages. Combined with our luxury beds and other facilities, Hotel Not Hotel offers an unrivalled experience. 
Besides all this, we feel that the value of a city lies in experiencing its local day-to-day life. As we are closely connected to Amsterdam and its numerous creative entrepreneurs, we know where to send you for a night on the town, a hidden culinary gem, or an upcoming artist’s exhibition. 

The bar connected to Hotel Not Hotel is called Kevin Bacon. Just like the actor, the bar has zero pretention, it always delivers, and it makes sure everybody has a good time.

Kevin Bacon is not your typical hotel bar. It’s where locals get their morning coffee or a quick breakfast. It’s where friends meet for a cocktail or a beer before diving into the nightlife, and where hotel guests let their hair down after a long day in the city.

When the weather is good, the ‘Whitey the White’ terrace is sunny like a perfectly fried egg. The staff’s friendly attitude shines all year round.

name: Historic Center Apartment
description: My place is close to The Central Station, the Dam square, the Nieuwmarkt, the Rembrandsplein,the Scheepvaartmuseum (Marine museum), Rembrandt Museum, Portuguese Synagogue, Flee market, Red Light District. Basically you are within walking distance to most attractions as well as being very near to metro, tram and buses. You’ll love my place because of the location, the ambiance and being in the inner heart of the historic center in a nice, relatively quiet street full of plants..
neighborhood_overview:
You will be in the historic inner city. This is where Amsterdam started, the very first city walls, entry gates, convents and all kind of activities started from here! The street you will be staying in, used to be part of a very big convent: the Bethaniën convent!! Hence the name of our street.

One of the nicest areas to see and visit during the day and the night. Great night live with quaint bars and lots of restaurants.
host_about:

I have lived myself in several other countries and love to travel. I also love music and books.

name: Amsterdam townhouse with garden in city center
description:
Welcome to our beautiful home in the heart of Amsterdam! Located in the charming and vibrant Staatsliedenbuurt neighborhood close to the Jordaan.
As you step inside our home, you'll be immediately struck by the warm and welcoming atmosphere. The house is full of light and features an open-plan layout, sunny garden, kitchen and 3 bedrooms. All in all, our home is the perfect choice for anyone looking for a comfortable and convenient base while exploring Amsterdam. We can't wait to welcome you!
neighborhood_overview:
The Staatsliedenbuurt is a vibrant residential neighborhood located in the west-central part of Amsterdam, just a short distance from the city center. It is a popular area for young professionals and families alike, with a diverse mix of residents from all walks of life.

The neighborhood is known for its charming, tree-lined streets and beautiful architecture, with many of the buildings dating back to the early 20th century. There are also plenty of green spaces in the area, including the Westerpark, which is a popular spot for jogging, picnicking, and attending cultural events.

In terms of amenities, the Staatsliedenbuurt has a lot to offer. There are plenty of restaurants, cafes, and bars to choose from, as well as a variety of shops and supermarkets. The neighborhood is also well-connected to public transportation, making it easy to reach other parts of the city.

description:
Our Nice room offers a 1.40 x 2.00 bed and a tiny private bathroom (toilet, shower, sink).

ATTENTION:
* You have to climb a spacesaving stairs to the room. Big luggage is difficult to bring up. Not practical if you have difficulties with stairs!

neighborhood_overview:
- Only 20 minutes to downtown Amsterdam by bus 21, tram 7 or bike.
- Metro stop De Vlugtlaan
- Within walking distance of Sloterdijk Station/Flixbus
- Close to Westerpark
- Easy metro from the Arena, Ziggodome, etc.
- Beautiful neighborhood just outside the ring with lots of greenery, low-rise buildings and playgrounds.
- Shopping center 5 minutes walk with daily market and supermarkets (open until 22h).
- Lots of restaurants nearby.
- Excellent transportation options to Schiphol, Sloterdijk Station, Downtown, Centraal Station and Zaanse Schans.
- Lots of nearby parks such as the Sloter Park with a beach, perfect jogging paths and outdoor training options.
- Rental of canoes and sailboats on the Sloterplas.
- Half an hour by train or bus 80 to Zandvoort/Amsterdam Beach/the North Sea.
host_about:
Welcome at Mi BnB, our B&B in Amsterdam West! We started in May 2017 and have had lots of guests from all over the world since then.

Mi BnB offers 2 simple rooms, the larger "Nice Room" on the 1st floor and the "Cozy Room" our tiny room on the ground floor. 

Each room has it's own small private bathroom, a smart tv,  coffee & tea facilities and a fridge. The 2 rooms share the front door and a small hall.

The B&B is in our family home in a nice, quiet, green neighbourhood, with excellent public transport around the corner. It is only half an hour to the city centre and all the tourist attractions are easy to reach by bus, tram or bike!

In Amsterdam it is only allowed to run a B&B when the owner lives in the house as well. So we are also living in the house with our children and our German Sheppard.

Please don't expect silence! At night it is quiet in general after 22h.

I love to meet new people from over the world and to speak foreign languages like English, Spanish, Italian and a little Portuguese, French and German.

We rent bikes for 10 euro per bike per day. 

Hope to meet you soon! 
Michèle  - Mi BnB

name: Canals & Cocktails: Courtyard view
description:
Welcome to Rosalia’s Menagerie, this modernized 17th-century canal house features a cocktail bar and six personally styled hotel rooms upstairs. Each a unique mix of style, charm and practicality.

Our cozier, quieter and cooler Garden View rooms (18m2) at the back of the building have king-sized bed and a view into a small and infamous Amsterdam inner courtyard area. They have bathrooms with rain showers and natural daylight. These rooms are equipped with fans and have windows on two sides.

On the principal floor you will find our cozy little cocktail bar inspired by Dutch heritage with its rich history in the trade, distilling, and innovation. We look forward to sharing our passion for Jenever and great locally-made spirits with you, as well as, showcase extraordinary products from abroad.

Upstairs you will find “Rosalia’s Inn Upstairs”, our lovely little hideaway. With six lovely rooms overlooking either the canal or a fetching Amsterdam secret
neighborhood_overview:
Tucked into Amsterdam's urban fabric, located on the Kloveniersburgwal Rosalia’s menagerie offers the city at your doorstep.

You will find the Nieuwmarkt square and Chinatown to the North, the University of Amsterdam and Amstel river to the South, the old Jewish Quarter to the East and the Dam and Red Light District to the West.

Rosalia's Menagerie is the perfect spot for anyone wanting to be in the center of the best Amsterdam has to offer regarding restaurants, cafes, museums, design, shopping and everything else. We are proud to be located on one of Amsterdam's older canals dating from the late 1400's.
host_about:
Rosalia's Menagerie is a lovely little hideaway located on the Kloveniersgburwal, close to the picturesque Nieuwmarkt Square, in the heart of the historic city centre of Amsterdam. We are proud to be located on one of Amsterdam's oldest canals dating from the late 1400's.

This modernized 17th-century canal house features six personally styled rooms and a world class cocktail bar. Each a unique mix of style, charm and practicality, overlooking either the canal or a fetching Amsterdam secret court yard garden. We offer exceptional hospitality for the cultural pilgrim, the hopeless romantic and weary business traveler alike.

Rosalia's Menagerie is a unique canal house with appealing rooms and a world class cocktail bar that becomes a home in Amsterdam for our guests.

description:

neighborhood_overview:
Neighbourhood: Plantage
A short cycle east of the city centre, Nieuwe Kerkstraat sits within the desirable Plantage district. Famous for its elegant squares and leafy boulevards, Amsterdam’s ‘cultural garden’ is packed with theatres, galleries, museums and parks, yet conveniently close to the thriving business district.
host_about: Wittenberg by Cove is a monumental short stay building, offering modern accommodation in Amsterdam's residential area. Carre Theater and the Dutch National Opera & Ballet are just 500 meters from the property. All other city highlights are easily accessible by pubic transport. Our neighborhood (the Plantage) is a small green village in the center of Amsterdam. There is a mix of people: students, families, the elderly, tourists, artists and writers. Artis is a one minute walk. In the morning you can hear the monkeys! The Hortus Botanicus is just as close.

The oldest center of Amsterdam is a 15-20 minute walk away. Our guests are very enthusiastic about the neighborhood, which is quiet, but very close to the center. Check the reviews!

name: Central & Comfy Home in Famous & Trendy De Pijp
description:
Want to experience Amsterdam like the locals do? My home offers you the unique experience to live in a typical home of an Amsterdammer in the most sought after area of our city and discover what it’s really about!

Central, in the heart of buzzing & trendy and De Pijp!

neighborhood_overview:
Lose yourself for hours in Amsterdam’s extremely instagrammable and lively Latin Quarter, with its colourful mix of cuisines, terraces and convivial cafés. From the beautiful landscaped gardens of Sarphatipark to the famous Albert Cuypmarkt street market, De Pijp oozes beatnik flair from every angle, and has been a popular haunt for creatives, students and bohemian spirits since the 60s.

The most famous and busiest street market of the Netherlands, the Albert Cuyp Market, is in De Pijp. It is open six days per week and attracts many tourists. The former Heineken brewery is also a popular tourist attraction. Next to the former Heineken brewery is the Marie Heinekenplein, which has a number of bars and cafes. Along the canal 'Ruysdaelkade' there is a small red light area.

De Pijp is densely populated and has a diverse population, with a relatively high percentage of highly educated people and people living alone. Famous Dutch people who have lived in De Pijp include
host_about:
Hi fellow traveler,
Thanks for taking the interest and time to read this :)

I am an engineer turned start-up advisor/entrepreneur who loves living in the coolest area of Amsterdam as much as I love traveling the globe, meeting amazing people and learning about cultures and languages!

I was born on the island of Cyprus, and lived on its gorgeous unspoilt Northern shores until 17. Then I moved around to study in the US, Istanbul and London before moving here a long time ago. Since then I've become a proud Amsterdammer! 

When I travel I prefer to have authentic experiences instead of being a mainstream tourist. If you are one of us who wants to live the real deal instead of following the flocks of tourists, then you have found your host! 
I try to befriend locals, learn and experience as much about the local history, language, culture and customs and try to be respectful and humble. I contribute if I can. When possible I give my money to the services of local hotels and businesses I want to support and to legit non-profits like a children's hospital or an animal shelter. 


In my free time I enjoy exploring the hidden history, architecture, world cuisine, art & design and nightlife of this great city.

My hobbies are good food in good company(I'm crazy about both cooking and dining out), going to musea, galleries & festivals, growing plants, ethics, photography, music & vinyl, history, activism, contemporary culture, travel, yoga and brewing beer. 

I am fascinated by our living planet and feel passionate about sustainability methods and practices whether it is my own life style choices, smart cities, permaculture, energy, technology, tourism, peace or ethical new world business models. 

I wish for a better world, not just for some but for all, and I know we have a tribe, people like you and me are out there :) I've seen that we can create our own reality together when we focus on a common goal together as a community and really connect, either physically or virtually through the web. (I can't help but chuckle now as I type this on airbnb!)

My guests would describe me as warm & friendly, hospitable, helpful, flexible and fun. I have probably thought of most things you might need during your stay!

As an insider I will give you tips on whatever you fancy - from where to indulge in the tastiest burger, local cuisine & delicacies, a Michelin starred dinner, or rooftop view cocktails, to what you shouldn't miss in town day and night... And more. 

I look forward to our paths crossing and welcoming you to Amsterdam!

With much love and best wishes, 
Ovgum

description: A 1960s bridge house perched between a neighbourhood park and a bustling intersection in Amsterdam North; a perfect overnight stop for cyclists on the LF7 cycle route. neighborhood_overview:
Meeuwenpleinbrug is located in Amsterdam North - a neighbourhood that has become increasingly popular over the past few years. With free ferries crossing the river IJ and the new North-South metro line up and running, it is faster and easier than ever to get to Amsterdam centre. But why would you, if there's so much to do and see in Meeuwenpleinbrug's vicinity? The relaxing Noorderpark is right next to your bridge, and eco-hub and restaurant De Ceuvel – one of Amsterdam's most innovative projects – is just around the corner. Check out our city guide for more recommendations!

This bridge house is classed as NOISE LEVEL - HIGH.
host_about:
SWEETS hotel is a one-of-a-kind hotel located across Amsterdam in 28 different bridge houses. Each bridge house has been transformed into an independent hotel suite that accommodates a maximum of two guests.

For centuries, Amsterdam’s bridge houses accommodated the city’s many bridge keepers who were responsible for opening the impressive structures for passing water traffic. With building dates between 1673 and 2009, the bridge houses carry an extensive history. Visiting them in chronological order is like a journey through time: their various architectural styles reveal how the city developed and grew, when new quarters were built and even how people thought about city development at that time.

With the hotel room interiors, SWEETS hotel offers guests a unique and comfortable living space, while keeping the history of every bridge house in mind. All bridge houses are different, and so are the interiors. Every SWEET accommodates up to two adults and contains a kingsize double bed, a bathroom and coffee and tea facilities.

SWEETS hotel, first initiated in 2012 as an urban space project, is 8 years in the making. Over half of the 28 bridge houses are currently available for reservations, with more coming soon.

SWEETS hotel is an initiative and co-creation of the architecture office Space&Matter (known for De Ceuvel, an ecological hub), project development partner Grayfield and Suzanne Oxenaar and Otto Nan of Seven New Things, founders of Lloyd Hotel & Cultural Embassy (the world’s first 1- to 5-star hotel) and Hotel The Exchange.

neighborhood_overview:
AMSTERDAM CENTRUM - CITY CENTER + CANAL DISTRICT
In addition to the world-famous historic Canal Ring, Amsterdam's City Centre district ('Centrum') is home to a host of renowned museums, charming and high-end shopping areas, fantastic culture and entertainment.
Amsterdam’s renowned art and culture and top shopping
In Amsterdam’s centre you will, of course, find the magnificent Canal Ring, but there is so much more besides. The inner city, for instance, is bursting with interesting museums, sometimes hidden behind beautiful canal-side façades. The diversity of the city centre’s culture is vast, from the emotional Anne Frank House in the west of the centre, through to the upbeat nature of Science Center NEMO in the east. In between, find an inspiring collection of art and history.
Top shopping
There is a vast array of shopping opportunities, from fashion brands and chains (Nieuwendijk, Kalverstraat) through to independent innovators and inspiring market stalls,

name: Sleeping in a Church - Room for 4 Family at BUNK
description: After just minutes on the free ferry from Central Station, you’ll find Amsterdam’s true north. This up-and-coming neighbourhood is alive and kicking, bursting with cultural hotspots. At the centre of the neighbourhood’s social life there was always the Saint Rita church. Now that it is home to Bunk Amsterdam, this still holds true. Locals and travellers alike come to catch some rays on our terrace, sticking around for an affordable dinner and some free cultural nourishment.
neighborhood_overview: Just minutes by free ferry from Central Station lies Amsterdam North. This neighbourhood is alive and kicking, bursting with cultural hotspots. Authentic urban life lives on, too, still undiscovered by tourists.The Saint Rita church has always been a place where the North comes together. Now, it’s also where you’ll find BUNK Amsterdam. This monumental building is now home to 296 guests, 106 rooms, 50 private pods, eight communal dinner tables and one teddy bear. So come to the North Side, where we have cookies, and company. On top of that, you’ll be sleeping in a church, a unique experience in Amsterdam!
host_about:
BUNK bridges the gap between hotels and hostels by combining affordable luxury rooms and smartly designed private bunk beds.
We converted two monumental churches into meeting places for travellers, locals and artists alike. We host inclusive social events in our sustainable restaurant and other communal spaces.

We aim to redefine hospitality by creating a place for communal wonderment. Where those who wander will start to wonder, triggered by true art, honest food and sincere smiles. We make comfort and design available to everyone, without the need for deep pockets. BUNK is where you can feel free to be your wonderfully weird self as you play your part in new stories you can take home with you.

neighborhood_overview:
Amsterdam is a city born for biking and walking. There are many sights to explore in the surroundings, such as the Flower market (50 m), Leidsestraat (220 m), Nieuwe Spiegelstraat art galleries (400 m), Rembrandtplein (450 m), The 9 Streets shopping district (650 m), Leidseplein (750 m), Royal Palace at Dam Square (850 m), the Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh Museum and Vondelpark (1 km) and the Anne Frank House (1,7 km). Central Station is located at 2 km, RAI Amsterdam (4,8 km and 15 min by taxi) and Schiphol Airport is at 15 km from the property.

The nearest supermarket (Albert Heijn at Koningsplein) is only 2 minutes away and the tram lines 1, 2 and 5 are the same distance. Trams 4, 9, 16 and 24 do stop at Muntplein and are at 3 minutes walking distance. In the center of the buzzing nightlife, several bars, LGBT venues, restaurants and two Pathé cinemas (including the historic Art Deco movie theater Tuschinski) can be found within a radius of 200 m.

neighborhood_overview: Numa Amsterdam Docklands is nestled in the area of Amsterdam’s Eastern Docklands, which once played a crucial role in trade with the Dutch East Indies. Today, it’s home to chic, Scandinavian waterfront architecture. The area is rich in culture and dining options, boasting many of Amsterdam's most authentic restaurants and bars. Whether you’re a nature lover or an enthusiastic urban explorer, Docklands offers the best of both worlds. Just a short stroll away, you’ll find captivating, natural attractions–Flevopark, Artis Zoo, and the breathtaking Hortus Botanical Garden, one of Amsterdam’s greatest gems. Close to the botanical garden, you'll find the vibrant heart of Amsterdam at Rembrandtplein. Here, the selection of locally brewed beers is endless, and the lively energy of Amsterdammers immaculate.
