neighborhood_overview: The Haarlemmerstraat and -dijk were declared the nicest shopping street in the Netherlands in 2011. Both streets together form a true gem located in the historical Jordaan area. The local residents consider it a small and cozy village, where everyone knows everyone. You will find all kinds of shops ranging from independent boutiques, specialized shops and small salons to trendy but affordable restaurants and bars and even the oldest cinema in Amsterdam, called the Movies. Famous buildings on the Haarlemmerstraat include the West Indisch Huis, where in 1625 the Dutch West India Company's governors ordered the construction of a fort on the island of Manhattan, laying the foundations for New York City. In short, this trendy and quirky street is perfect for spending hours roaming, shopping and dining. The shopping night falls on Thursday with stores open until 9:00 pm.

name: floating studio nescio
description:
Private Water studio Nescio on a private luxury houseboat. Not far from city center on a unique water and forrest side with great panoramic views.
15 minutes walk from the subway station "Noorderpark" 1 stop from Amsterdam central station. If you rent bikes at a bike rental you can bike in 13 minutes to the ferry (IJplein) who will to central station with you bike.
neighborhood_overview:
Amsterdam North is reachable by a free ferry from/to the city center, the ferry goes every 10 minutes if you by bicycle.
Or you can take the subway to central Station or further to the heart of the city. The subway station Noorderpark is 15 minute walk from the houseboat.

Amsterdam North is a rising creative neighbourhood on the other side of the IJ river, where the Eye museum (cinema museum) is located and a several incrowd restaurants, bars, for example the Goudfazant, Skate cafe, Coba and former NDSM shipyard (restaurants/club , flea market and other events).
host_about: I am working as a freelancer in streetcasting for commercials and photography. I live over 20 years in Amsterdam now, for the last 8 years I live on this fabulous houseboat which is a little paradise surrounded by forrest and of course water. The houseboat and the surrounding is very precious to me and I like to share this hidden paradise with people who can appreciate such a special place. To give an Amsterdam experience on a different way means a lot to me.

neighborhood_overview:
This beautiful apartment can be found in the south of the city to the immediate west of the beautiful Amstelpark. The park is the perfect place to enjoy a picnic and grab some quiet time with a good book under any of the thousands of tall trees. The park also incorporates a restaurant, a playground, some galleries, and a mini golf course known locally as ‘midget golf’. Make your way through the park to discover a beautiful authentic windmill alongside the stunning River Amstel. Why not hire bicycles and cycle across the park emerging alongside the river? You can follow it south to the pretty town of Ouder Amstel. On your way there you will pass through open countryside, experience beautiful wildlife and breathe lots of fresh air. Enjoy a beer and an ice cream and cycle back into along the opposite side of the river. It makes an excellent afternoon excursion for all the family.

The famous 9 streets (9 Straatjes) is only a 2-minute walk away. Here you will find all kinds of small shops, cafes and restaurants. neighborhood_overview:
The East district is cozy and small-scale. Many authentic shops, super tasty coffees, delicious ice cream and good restaurants. The Wibaut campus and Roeters Eiland serve 60.000 students. Which leads to a lot of activity and the district is very diverse. You can see the beautiful Oosterpark from my apartment.
I can heartily recommend the various restaurants in my area. Especially Oost Italian restaurant below my apartment has excellent pizzas. I go to Park café-restaurant in Oosterpark for breakfast, lunch or delicious dinners. The best bakery in Amsterdam is Hartog's Volkoren, nearby at Wibautstraat 77. At Campertstraat 48/50, the barista makes excellent coffees and they bake delicious patisserie.
The Beukenplein is one of the nicest squares in Amsterdam East! You will find Coffee Bru, various cafes and restaurants, also Ekodis: a unique organic shop. Eriks Delicatessen sells every cheese you are looking for, good wine, and asparagus from Limburg in season, crispy fresh bre
host_about: I lived in Amsterdam for twenty years now and it still feels like I'm on a holiday. I like to visit the beautiful museums and walk along it's canals and parks. I watch a lot of movies in the beautiful cinema's. Also I listen classical music and visit concerts at the world famous Amsterdam Concert Building. You'll often find me walking in nature, like the Eastern Park. I'm vegetarian, love kundalini yoga and dedicated to my spiritual journey as a student of the Diamond Approach in Ridhwan school.

neighborhood_overview:
Spiegelkwartier

The Spiegelkwartier is the Art and Antiques district in Amsterdam.
Here you’ll find more than 70 antiques dealers and art galleries, together offering a resplendent array of fine historical objects, classic and contemporary art, and exotic folk art.
You will find also restaurants, casual eateries and cosy cafés on almost every corner.
The Spiegelkwartier is located between the Herengracht and the Rijksmuseum.

www.spiegelkwartier.nl

Museum Quarter

The Amsterdam Museum Quarter is home to the three most important museums in the Netherlands: the Rijksmuseum, the Stedelijk Museum and the Van Gogh Museum.
It also houses the majestic Concertgebouw and some superb cafés and restaurants.
With its large grassy open area, the Museumplein offers plenty of room to have a picnic or just relax. The Museum Quarter even has its own indoor swimming pool.

name: 18th Century Groundfloor Canal House with patio
description: With its picturesque canal view, rich history and modern design, this 2 floor house is a unique city destination. Each area of Amsterdam has its own character and charm. neighborhood_overview:
The house is located on one of the major residential canals in the city centre. It is conveniently located near major tram-lines and only steps away from the Anne Frank House, Dam Square and the Jordaan, which is famous for its variety of shops, restaurants and galleries. The major museums: Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh and Stedelijk are within walking distance. Traveling with children? Visit NEMO (the largest science centre in the Netherlands), ARTIS Royal Zoo, Kinderkookcafé in the Vondelpark, Madame Tussauds or outside Amsterdam De Efteling, Dolfinarium or Madurodam. 
“A Narrow Strip of Dutch Cool”
That is what The New York Times called the Herenstraat and surrounded area. The neighbourhood became increasingly trendy (…) the quaint streets of Herenstraat and Prinsenstraat began to buzz with new energy. Trendy bars and innovative restaurants have opened. And a steady march of independent boutiques is turning the area into one of the city’s coolest retail strips.
host_about:

We hope you have a great stay at one of the most beautiful places in town.

Regards,
Saskia and Michiel

neighborhood_overview: De Pijp, a vibrant neighborhood in Amsterdam, offers an array of attractions within close proximity. Just steps away, you'll find the famous Albert Cuyp Market, where you can immerse yourself in local culture and indulge in street food. If you're in the mood for nature, Sarphatipark is a charming green oasis perfect for a leisurely stroll or a picnic. For beer enthusiasts, the Heineken Experience is a short distance away, offering an interactive museum and beer tasting sessions. Art lovers can explore the neighborhood's thriving art scene, with numerous galleries showcasing contemporary works. De Pijp is also a paradise for foodies, with its diverse culinary scene, ranging from cozy cafes to trendy restaurants. Additionally, you can enjoy the convenience of easy access to popular attractions like the Concertgebouw, known for its classical music performances, and the Museum Square. With its central location and vibrant atmosphere, De Pijp promises a memorable experience in Amsterdam.

name: Quiet Gem, lovely B&B in the Heart of Amsterdam
description: Independent B&B on our houseboat with your own entrance. We are located on a sunny and quiet canal in the heart of Amsterdam, close to Centraal Station, Anne Frank House, The Jordaan and the Canals. Your space is totally private with your own bathroom, bedroom, captain's room and wheel house. The space is centrally heated and double glazed for chilly days. You also have access to outside space on our pier where you can relax into the evening on warm summer nights.
neighborhood_overview: We live within walking distance to the best  shops, bars, restaurants and weekly farmers markets. It's central, yet a very quiet and relaxing neighborhood with a great mix of new and historic, artists and creatives and a great pub serving artisan beers just at the end of our street.
host_about:
We are a Dutch couple (I am originally American) who love to travel and meet people, so having a b&b is a great way to bring the world closer to us! We were introduced to each other on a hot summer's day at a beach party at Blijburg by Jake's dog Max (who was on the planet for over 14 years)  Life was never the same. 

We now have Akasha, (or should I say she has us!) a very pretty young dog rescued from the woods in southern Italy by a couple on holiday. 

Living on the water has always been a dream for us, and 10 years ago we made it a reality. We are so lucky to have the best neighbours and a great local community of caring people around us. Cooking for friends and practicing yoga are dear to my heart as well as my appreciation for Dutch art and design. Jake has his own interactive design agency in town and cycles to work. I am currently working on two MAs at the University of Amsterdam, one in philosophy, the other in English Literature and Culture.

name: Sabine and Walter Euler apartment
description: My apartment is at walking distance to all mayor attractions: the trendy shopping area the 9 Streets, museums, restaurants, supermarkets. You will enjoy our comfortable place, with beautiful art. The building is situated in a quiet corner of the city center. We want our guests to have quality time! neighborhood_overview: The neighbourhood is the most exciting part of Amsterdam. Most of the cultural, entertainment and shopping facilities are in immediate distance. Nevertheless, the building is situated in a quiet corner.
host_about:
H401 is the name of a 17th century canal house in the very centre of Amsterdam at one of the most beautiful canal conjunctions. The house has a stunning history as a hiding place during WWII, but other than the Anne Frank house the building is still used for living and working . In their space H401 features a unique high end cultural programme based on the history of the house that has always been a magnet for artists, writers and people on the move. Castrum Peregrini was the code name during the war and literally translates as 'castle of the pilgrim'. The original hiding place is still preserved and the various floors and spaces represent the houses vibrant history since the 1940s.

Three friends, Michael, Frans and Lars run H401 today. The building contains partly a time capsule going back to the forties / fifties / sixties and combines it with contemporary spaces.  In this typical Amsterdam canal house, we offer two independent guest appartments, both recently restored in a minimalist manner, with a stunning view to the canal in the front  facing a row of historic 17th century gables and the monumental Krijtberg Church in the back. The rooms offer the unique experience of a historic place with contemprary comfort (state of the art beds, linen, towels etc) The apartments offer fully equipped kitchens  with dishwasher, microwave/ oven etc. Though the house is situated at a quiete part of the famous 'gouden bocht' of the Herengracht, the famous lively and fashionable nine streets are literraly around the corner; they offer stylish cafes, bars, shops, lunch and diner possibilities. All famous sites and museums are on walking distance. Amsterdam at its very best!

The Jordaan area and canals are nothing but highlights. Throughout the world-famous canals maze-like streets offer hidden courtyards. Tons of nice restaurants and clubs (Salmuera, Lion Noir, Palladium, Jimmy Woo, Chicago Social Club, Chin Chin Club).

Within a three-min walking distance from he Anne Frank house, the Wester Church, the Dam Square and the famous Noordermarkt (biological food market each Saturday and textile market each Monday).

Around ten-min walking distance you will find the Museum Square, the Van Gogh Museum and the Vondelpark.

Enjoy the little design boutiques with furniture, clothes and jewellery on the well known ''9 straatjes'' (9 streets).

The apartment is located in de so called ''Indische Buurt''. It is a neighbourhood filled with cosy restaurants, bars, cinema etc. All kinds of other shops are nearby.
The Dappermarkt is one of the country’s oldest - and best - outdoor markets. It forms the multicultural heart of the neighbourhood along with the hip and happening Javastraat, which once only provided every manner of global grocery but is now beginning to feature more upscale cafes and terraces. One of the city’s largest general hospitals, OLVG, is also located here. Two stellar city parks, Oosterpark and Flevopark, provide quick escape from the relatively busy streets. Besides many cheap ethnic eateries and supermarkets, the area is attracting much more higher-end cafes and restaurants who are often reinventing many of the area’s industrial monuments. On sunny days, the parks and terraces are filled with locals.

neighborhood_overview:
Spiegelkwartier

The Spiegelkwartier is the Art and Antiques district in Amsterdam.
Here you’ll find more than 70 antiques dealers and art galleries, together offering a resplendent array of fine historical objects, classic and contemporary art, and exotic folk art.
You will find also restaurants, casual eateries and cosy cafés on almost every corner.
The Spiegelkwartier is located between the Herengracht and the Rijksmuseum.

www.spiegelkwartier.nl

Museum Quarter

The Amsterdam Museum Quarter is home to the three most important museums in the Netherlands: the Rijksmuseum, the Stedelijk Museum and the Van Gogh Museum.
It also houses the majestic Concertgebouw and some superb cafés and restaurants.

name: Design Studio in Amsterdam Center!
description:
Studio Highlights:
★ Located in the City Center
★ Near the Amsterdam famous Canals
★ 10-15 min walking distance to the Anne Frank House
★ 10 minute walking distance from Amsterdam Central Station
★ Cable TV
★ Fast Wi-Fi
★ Coffee and tea facilities
neighborhood_overview:
The studio is located in Haarlemmerbuurt - a lively neighbourhood, next to the City Centre. Close to the apartment you can find the Haarlemmerstraat and Haarlemmerdijk streets, known for the great quality and diversity of their shops, boutiques and restaurants.
Moreover, the district borders with the Jordaan neighbourhood in the south - buzzing place with a lot of old Dutch cafes, great restaurants and brick oven pizzerias, boutique design shops, local supermarkets and sight-seeings, such as Westerkerk and the Anne Frank House.

From the apartment you can also easily reach the City Centre and its most famous sight-seeing: the Dam square with the Royal Palace and some famous Amsterdam museums.

Come and enjoy this lovely studio and the great neighbourhood!
host_about:
Hey, my name is Rutger. I'm 31 years old and I have lived in Amsterdam my entire life. I have nearly finished my law studies, hoping to graduate as a Msc in Criminal Law next year. I've been running a B&B for quite some time now. I live in one of the three rooms we have. 

Around the corner you will be amazed by the view of the Nieuwe Prinsengracht and the Westertoren. To experience Amsterdam in all its glory, you're at the right place at B&B 305. Enjoy the liveliness of the city when you step out, and take a comfortable break when you step in. I'm sure you will enjoy your stay at B&B 305!

name: Amsterdam Blossom Houseboat
description: Docked only a few minutes from Amsterdam Central Station, newly renovated and designed under architecture: that's the Amsterdam Blossom. You will be sleeping in the hull of a historical freight ship, in a bed that is basically a throne. The houseboat studio offers lots of space and light getting in via the many stylish portholes. And the location could not be better: close to the buzzing city centre, yet you are assured to enjoy a good night rest since our harbor is peaceful.
neighborhood_overview:
Although our harbor is nice and quiet, you will be in the middle of the city life within a minute walking. Across the street you find one of the many great bars around: Café Pollux, laid back place to have a drink or simple meal. Walk on and you will find many other places to eat, with cuisines basically from every corner of the world. In the immediate neighborhood you will find the buzzing Zeedijk and red light district, as well as the Maritime and Science museums. Get inspired on the daily market Nieuwmarkt and find cool stuff on the Waterloo Square (Waterlooplein). Walk the other way and you will enjoy the amazing views over the IJ-River, and when the sun is shining make sure to visit the terrace of bar Hannekes Boom! 

Well, I hope to have given the best possible impression possible, but don't hesitate to ask if you have any questions -always glad to help. Look forward welcoming you aboard!

Amsterdam is the place to be for Rembrandts, Van Goghs and modern art. The theatres present shows mainly in Dutch off course, but opera and ballet are universal languages. description:
Enjoy some extra leg room with one of our Queensized bunks within a cosy shared dorm at the Flying Pig Downtown Hostel. Our 18th century building has been transformed into one of the most notorious party spots in the Dutch capital. Complete with reading lights, USB ports and lockers under your bed to store your things, this is the perfect spot for young backpackers to base themselves.

host_about:
Our Flying Pig Downtown hostel is the place to party in Amsterdam with the 18th century building having been transformed into one of the most notorious party spots in the Dutch capital. We host awesome DJ’s and epic themed parties boasting an indoor smoking area with a pillow stage! 

Located in the heart of the city centre next to Amsterdam Central Station, you will also find Dam Square, The Anne Frank House and the bustling Red Light District just a short stroll away. Our hostel also provides free use of the guest kitchen, luggage storage, free Wi-Fi, a book exchange and a buffet style continental breakfast available everyday for only €5!

Known for our wild nights and a relaxed atmosphere during the day, we’re a popular spot for young backpackers to base themselves. There’s a reason we were rated the Best Hostel in the Netherlands in 2013 by TripAdvisor!

name: Secret Garden Studio
description:
To ultimate relaxation in a city where there is always something to do?

The bright studio has a private entrance and is located on a small “Japanese” courtyard garden. When you open the sliding door, you are in the garden.

Our house is located on a garden along the Papaver Canal where you can swim.
We have made a list of places where you can have breakfasts, food, etc., the gems of North. If you want to go downtown, take the bus and get downtown in 15 minutes. Or you can walk in 12 minutes to the free ferry that will take you across the IJ to Central Station.

name: Albert Cuyp III A Studio Amsterdam
description: Modern studio apartment, housing up to two people in Amsterdam’s Pijp district, overlooking the vibrant Albert Cuyp Market.
neighborhood_overview:
The accommodation can be found south of the historical centre, lining one of the country’s most famous market streets. Existing over a 100 years, the Albert Cuypmarkt attracts a diverse crowd and enjoys a bustling yet laid back atmosphere, making it the perfect introduction to typical Amsterdam life. This animated attraction is located in the Pijp quarter, renowned for its multicultural population and its multitude of friendly bars, cafes and restaurants, providing more ethnic eateries than any other part of the city.


The studio is superbly placed for discovering Amsterdam’s stunning Canal Belt and its equally picturesque river, the Amstel. Accessible on foot in a matter of minutes, this elegant waterway hosts several cultural venues along its banks, including the Royal Carre Theatre and the Hermitage Museum. The capital’s famous Dam Square can be reached on foot in half an hour or by tram in less than fifteen minutes.

description:
A beautifully located, spacious (120m2) comfortable family home with a view on a green park. It is situated close to the city centre (8 min) and near to RAI. It has 3 bedrooms, a big living room and a kitchen. Kick back and relax in the sunny garden incl. a picnic table, a 'kids castle'...and a trampoline!
The popular 'De Pijp' is just a stone's throw away from the apartment. Bus stops, metro, restaurants, green parks and grocery stores are around the corner.
neighborhood_overview:
Our apartment is located in one of the most popular areas of Amsterdam called 'Rivierenbuurt'/'De Pijp'. Rivierenbuurt literally translates to ‘River neighborhood’. It's name stems from the fact that most of the streets here are named after rivers in the Netherlands. This residential neighborhood was developed in the 1920s for Amsterdam’s middle class. It was part of architect Hendrik Petrus Berlage's ‘Plan Zuid’, and was considered quite an architectural wonder in those days. Even today, architecture buffs find their way around the many examples of Amsterdam School architecture.

De Pijp is one of Amsterdam's most popular neighborhoods. Lose yourself for hours in this lively Latin Quarter of Amsterdam, with its colorful mix of cuisines, terraces and convivial cafes. From the beautiful landscaped gardens of Sarphatipark to the famous Albert Cuypmarkt street market, De Pijp oozes beatnik flair from every angle.

Our apartment is also extremely suitable for visitors
host_about:
Like most people on Airbnb we love to travel and discover new places. We like the concept of enjoying your stay local style very much. It's how we prefer to travel ourselves and experience local life abroad. Besides that we love cooking, art, theatre, cinema, skiing, biking and friends & family. 

We are the proud hosts of a nice apartment in the city centre on the edge of the most beautiful and famous park of Amsterdam: The Vondelpark. Everything this magic city has to offer is just around our corner. 

Being travellers ourselves, we do our ultimate best to give you a great Amsterdam experience. You will find all basics available (coffee, tea, juice, cookies) and all information relating to travel systems, restaurant tips etc.

If you have an early arrival or late departure, no problem. We are not a hotel and so are very flexible! And if you have any questions, feel free to ask. We love Amsterdam, know the 'off the beaten track', local places and can help you find your way in Amsterdam. We hope you enjoy the city as much as we do!

A newly (2015) developed area surrounded by old and famous neighbourhoods:

Watergraafsmeer, typical East-Amsterdam area with great boutique shopping and high quality stores combined with a lot of playgrounds.

Don't get me wrong, this is not a suburban area, there's a very active and interesting night life with bars and restaurants. Some of the best restaurants in town are right around the corner.

Indische buurt: Hipstertown. Go to the javastraat and bar hop or sit down for a bite anywhere. It's all good.

Oosterpark: large city park, just renovated. Surrounded by bars and restaurants. You will pass this when you got to the Zoo "Artis", which is 5 minutes by tram.

Flevopark: fantastic park with a recently renovated playground. Next to the park is an outdoor swimming pool (Flevobad).

name: Sonder Park House | Double Room
description:
Museums, thrift shopping, and stroopwafels — your Amsterdam experience begins at Park House. Each room includes a coffee maker, space to work, and an HDMI-equipped television for streaming. To unwind, we love the cozy lounge and calming garden terrace.

In the Oud-Zuid neighborhood, there’s plenty to discover. The iconic Rijksmuseum is home to the best of Dutch art. And Vondelpark hosts seasonal outdoor concerts at the Openluchttheater. Enjoy a picturesque stay at Park House.
neighborhood_overview: Oud-Zuid is the best-kept secret in Amsterdam. And we're giving you the scoop. Bordering the historic center, this trendy neighborhood has the ideal combination of upscale boutiques and memorable museums. Spend your day relaxing in the beautiful greenery of Vondelpark, listening to music spilling from the open-air theater. Or stroll down charming tree-lined streets to admire the stunning 19th-century mansions. Ready for some shopping? Check out P.C. Hooftstraat. Formally named after a Dutch poet and playwright, this luxurious street is home to countless designer stores including Gucci and Dior. After a day of discovery, indulge in one of the many global restaurants and cuisines scattered throughout.
host_about: Rooms, suites, and apartments in over 40 cities around the world. Every Sonder features designer details, keyless entry, and fast free WiFi. Experience a better way to stay today.
